long_description:
VSIs (0717_aa64f1b2-8b38-4370-b8e4-53991b451f0b and 0717_e3ccce31-c4e3-49ad-afaa-450e67473651) failed to start with error during migration, “There is not enough capacity to schedule this VM within the Placement Group"

These VM's are in stopped state, as they couldn't be migrated.
Now the capacity is available, so should be start the VM's to bring them back to active state.

VSI IDs:

0717_aa64f1b2-8b38-4370-b8e4-53991b451f0b
0717_e3ccce31-c4e3-49ad-afaa-450e67473651

current_status_next_steps:
To add members        "dal1-qz1-sr3-rk197-s18",  "dal1-qz1-sr3-rk197-s19" to placement group:

kubectl -n fmfwtool patch placementgroup migrateall \
  --type=merge \
  -p '{
    "spec": {
      "members": [
        "dal1-qz1-sr3-rk197-s04",
        "dal1-qz1-sr3-rk197-s12",
        "dal1-qz1-sr3-rk197-s18",
        "dal1-qz1-sr3-rk197-s19"
      ]
    }
  }'


Need to start VM's to see if that fixed. If it fails again, For failed VM's in the placement group, remove the reference to the placement group:


VM #1

kubectl patch virtualmachine 0717_e3ccce31-c4e3-49ad-afaa-450e67473651 \
  -n 4b3e5a49a7ab4432a8bd6e3fa571b5d8 \
  --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "remove", "path": "/spec/operator/placementGroups"}]'


VM #2

kubectl patch virtualmachine 0717_aa64f1b2-8b38-4370-b8e4-53991b451f0b \
  -n 4b3e5a49a7ab4432a8bd6e3fa571b5d8 \
  --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "remove", "path": "/spec/operator/placementGroups"}]'
